_meta.title: Duet / RepRapFirmware — tools[] toolchanger model + active-tool + heater deref
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (Object-Model docs + LGPL @duet3d/objectmodel); toolchange-status live behaviour ⚪ needs an E3D ToolChanger/Jubilee owner-report

derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from Duet3D Object-Model docs. RRF is a toolchanger LEADER — tools[] is a first-class array, a far cleaner source than scraping a status object.

notes: RRF has NO per-slot MMU/feeder model — multi-tool is a TOOLHEAD concept, orthogonal to a feeder. Report no multi-material for core RRF; never fabricate slots. See ../../patterns/multi-material-feeders.md.
There is NO tools[n].filament field — the only filament signal is a NAME on the extruder (move.extruders[i].filament), resolved for a tool via tools[n].filamentExtruder (an extruder index).
Detect a toolchanger by PRESENCE (tools[].length > 1 with independent offsets/heaters), never a vendor string.
Heaters and bed/chamber are behind an INDEX dereference — resolve indices first (bed is not always heater 0).

fields.name: string | null
fields.active: float[] — already-resolved active setpoints (°C); use directly
fields.heaters: int[] — indices into heat.heaters[]; heat.heaters[heaters[0]].current = primary hotend temp
fields.offsets: float[] — per-axis offset ordered X, Y, Z, ...
fields.filamentExtruder: int — the extruder index whose .filament NAME applies to this tool
fields.spindle: int | -1 — maps a tool to a CNC spindle (out of FFF scope)

active_tool.note: authoritative — do NOT scan per-tool state; fold -1 to 'none'

heater_deref.comment: temps are °C but roles are indices into heat.heaters[]

heater_fields.state: off | standby | active | fault | tuning | offline

heater_deref.safety: a heater.state == 'fault' is a real safety signal worth surfacing

macros_note: A T<n> triggers RRF's user-authored tfree<n>.g / tpre<n>.g / tpost<n>.g macros on SD; a client only issues T<n> (or `T<n> P0` to suppress them) — it does not author them.
